25.reason: This else is attached only to the last if, causing incorrect handling when the first two if conditions are false but the third is also false (e.g., mixed types like B-e). It should not be here; instead, the else should be part of the outer if-else structure.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  This else is attached only to the last if, causing incorrect handling when the first two if conditions are false but the third is also false (e.g., mixed types like B-e). It should not be here; instead, the else should be part of the outer if-else structure.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  This else is attached only to the last if, causing incorrect handling when the first two if conditions are true but the third is false (e.g., mixed types like B-e). It should not be here; the else should be part of a proper if-else chain or removed.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This else belongs only to the last if, causing incorrect handling when types do not match (e.g., "B-e"). It should output the dash and surrounding characters unchanged.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  This else is attached only to the third if condition (lowercase check). When the characters are of different types (e.g., 'B' and 'e'), none of the three type-specific ifs are true, so this else executes. It incorrectly increments i and copies ch[i] (the character after the dash) without copying the dash itself, causing the dash to be omitted from output.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  This 'else' is incorrectly attached only to the lowercase check instead of the overall expansion condition, causing it to trigger for mismatched types.

6.reason: Loop should be i<strlen(ch) to avoid out-of-bounds access and processing null terminator.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Loop should be i<strlen(ch) to avoid out-of-bounds access and processing null terminator.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Loop runs one step too far: `i <= strlen(ch)` accesses null terminator and beyond, causing out-of-bounds read when checking ch[i+1]]

7.reason: Condition does not check that both characters belong to the same character type (lowercase, uppercase, or digit); for input "B-e" it enters this block even though 'B' (uppercase) and 'e' (lowercase) are of different types.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Missing type check (both characters must be same type) and lack of check for left < right-1? Also, ch[i-1] and ch[i+1] may be out of bounds when i==0 or i==strlen.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Condition does not check that both characters belong to the same character type (lowercase, uppercase, or digit); for input "B-e" it enters this block even though 'B' (uppercase) and 'e' (lowercase) are of different types.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Checks character order before verifying they are of the same type, allowing mismatched types like 'B' and 'e' to enter the block.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Does not verify that characters around '-' are of the same type before comparing; also accesses ch[i-1] when i=0 (undefined behavior)]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This condition accesses ch[i-1] and ch[i+1] without checking if i is at the start or end of the string, leading to out-of-bounds access. Also, it doesn't ensure both sides are of the same character type before comparing.]

14.reason: Should be 'else if' to form a proper mutually exclusive conditional chain.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  Should be 'else if' to form a proper mutually exclusive conditional chain.

26.reason: [This 'i++' skips the next character after '-', which is incorrect. When expansion conditions aren't met (e.g., mixed case like B-e), the '-' and following character should be copied as-is, not skipped. Also, this 'else' only applies to the last 'if', not all three type checks.]
